nodes.name: Sticky Note4

parameters.content:
## 3: Using the Custom Workflow Tool
[Read more about Workflow Triggers]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

One common implementation of tool use is to set them up as workflows which are intended triggered via other workflows. With this, we can either build a tool per workflow or for efficiency, take an API approach where multiple tools can exist behind a router (in this case our "switch" node).

Our AI agent will therefore only passing through the parameters of the request and won't have to learn/know how to intereact directly with the tools and services.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Create Calendar Event1
nodes.notes: This googleCalendar node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note6

parameters.content:
## Try It Out!
### This workflow does the following:
* Retrieves a meeting transcript
* Sends transcript to an AI Agent to parse and carry out follow up actions if necessary.
* If transcript mentions a follow up meeting is required, the AI Agent will call a tool to create the meeting.
* Additionally if able, the AI Agent will also assign attendees it thinks should attend the meeting. 

### Need Help?
Join the [Discord]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} or ask in the [Forum]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Happy Hacking!

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Schedule Meeting

parameters.name: create_calendar_event

nodes.notes: This toolWorkflow node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: AI Agent

parameters.text:
=system: your role is to help people get the most out of their meetings. You achieve this by helpfully summarising the meeting transcript to pull out useful information and key points of interest and delivery this in note form. You also help carry out any follow-up actions on behalf of the meeting attendees.
1. Summarise the meeting and highlight any key goals of the meeting.
2. Identify and list important points mentioned by each attendee. If non-applicable for an attendee, skip and proceed to the next attendee.
3. Identify and list all next steps agreed by the attendees. If there are none, make a maximum of 3 suggestions based on the transcript instead. Please list the steps even if they've already been actioned.
4. identify and perform follow-up actions based on a transcript of a meeting. These actions which are allowed are: creating follow-up calendar events if suggested by the attendees.

The meeting details were as follows:
* The creator of the meeting was {{ $('Get Calendar Event').item.json["creator"]["displayName"] }} <{{ $('Get Calendar Event').item.json["creator"]["email"]}}>
* The attendees were {{ $('Get Calendar Event').item.json["attendees"].map(attendee => `${attendee.display_name} <${attendee.email}>`).join(', ') }}
* The meeting was scheduled for {{ $('Get Calendar Event').item.json["start"]["dateTime"] }}

The meeting transcript as follows:
```
{{ $json["text"] }}
```

nodes.notes: This agent node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note5

parameters.content:
## 1. Get Calendar Event
[Read more about working with Google Calendar]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

In this demo, we've decided to go with google meet as transcripts are stored in the user google drive. First, we'll need to get the calendar event of which the google meet was attached.
If the meet was not arranged through Google calendar, you may need to skip this step and just reference the transcripts in google drive directly.

* Set your calendar event ID here.

parameters.text:
=system: your role is to help people get the most out of their meetings. You achieve this by helpfully summarising the meeting transcript to pull out useful information and key points of interest and delivery this in note form. You also help carry out any follow-up actions on behalf of the meeting attendees.
1. Summarise the meeting and highlight any key goals of the meeting.
2. Identify and list important points mentioned by each attendee. If non-applicable for an attendee, skip and proceed to the next attendee.
3. Identify and list all next steps agreed by the attendees. If there are none, make a maximum of 3 suggestions based on the transcript instead. Please list the steps even if they've already been actioned.
4. identify and perform follow-up actions based on a transcript of a meeting. These actions which are allowed are: creating follow-up calendar events if suggested by the attendees.

The meeting details were as follows:
* The creator of the meeting was {{ $('Get Calendar Event').item.json["creator"]["displayName"] }} <{{ $('Get Calendar Event').item.json["creator"]["email"]}}>
* The attendees were {{ $('Get Calendar Event').item.json["attendees"].map(attendee => `${attendee.display_name} <${attendee.email}>`).join(', ') }}
* The meeting was scheduled for {{ $('Get Calendar Event').item.json["start"]["dateTime"] }}

The meeting transcript as follows:
```
{{ $json["text"] }}
```

parameters.name: create_calendar_event

parameters.content:
## 4. This Tool Creates Calendar Events
This tool, given event details and a list of attendees, will create a new Google calendar event and add the attendees to it.

🚨**Required**
* Set your calendar event ID here.

## 3: Using the Custom Workflow Tool
[Read more about Workflow Triggers]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

One common implementation of tool use is to set them up as workflows which are intended triggered via other workflows. With this, we can either build a tool per workflow or for efficiency, take an API approach where multiple tools can exist behind a router (in this case our "switch" node).

Our AI agent will therefore only passing through the parameters of the request and won't have to learn/know how to intereact directly with the tools and services.

## 1. Get Calendar Event
[Read more about working with Google Calendar]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

In this demo, we've decided to go with google meet as transcripts are stored in the user google drive. First, we'll need to get the calendar event of which the google meet was attached.
If the meet was not arranged through Google calendar, you may need to skip this step and just reference the transcripts in google drive directly.

## Try It Out!
### This workflow does the following:
* Retrieves a meeting transcript
* Sends transcript to an AI Agent to parse and carry out follow up actions if necessary.
* If transcript mentions a follow up meeting is required, the AI Agent will call a tool to create the meeting.
* Additionally if able, the AI Agent will also assign attendees it thinks should attend the meeting. 

### Need Help?
Join the [Discord]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} or ask in the [Forum]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Happy Hacking!

### 💡 Can't find your transcript?
Only meetings which own and were recorded and had transcription enabled will be available.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.

Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.

testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
